pred: The GhostVLAD approach is a pooling method that was originally proposed for face recognition, but is also used in this paper for language identification. It is an extension of the VLAD (Vector of Locally Aggregated Descriptors) approach, which is a pooling method that aggregates local features into a single global feature. In the GhostVLAD approach, additional "ghost" clusters are added to the VLAD clusters, which helps to improve the robustness of the method to noise and outliers. The ghost
answers: extension of the NetVLAD, adds Ghost clusters along with the NetVLAD clusters
An extension of NetVLAD which replaces hard assignment-based clustering with soft assignment-based clustering with the additon o fusing Ghost clusters to deal with noisy content.
